snippet.title: Oil Hit $91. The S&P Sold Its Bounce Anyway.
snippet.description:
Oil touched $91 on the ninth day of US strikes on Iran — and the S&P still sold its bounce from the opening print to the bell.

Post-close read of Monday, July 20 on settled prints: the Houthi naval blockade declared against Saudi Arabia, Brent's fade off 91.42, XLE's breakout over 57.30, the Nasdaq 100's broken floor, and the 10-year pressing its July high. Charts are TradingView settled daily bars; sources: Reuters via US News, NPR, EIA, CNBC, and the Federal Reserve calendar.

Three cases off the close, each with its invalidation. The two lines that decide Tuesday: S&P 7,421.82 and the 10-year yield at 4.64.

Research tooling, not financial advice. No performance is promised or implied.
